MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-31-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Joseph Alen Banks Jr's motion to quash is denied.

Plaintiff Banks alleges he was assaulted at the Embarcadero Bart Station, then without cause arrested by Bart Police officers. He is suing BART and various officers for wrongful arrest. On June 2, 2025, Defendants filed a cross-complaint against Larry Strickland, alleging Strickland was the person who assaulted Plaintiff. Defendants seek indemnity. Strickland is the only defendant named in the cross-complaint. Now, Plaintiff moves to quash service of the cross-complaint.

Plaintiff does not have standing to move to quash service of the summons and cross-complaint because he is not named as a defendant in the cross-complaint. "A defendant, on or before the last day of his or her time to plead...may serve and file a notice of motion...[t]o quash service of summons on the ground of lack of jurisdiction of the court over him or her." (Code of Civil Procedure section 418.10.) Plaintiff is not a defendant for purposes of the complaint or the cross-complaint. Thus, he is not able to make a motion to quash service of the cross-complaint. Moreover, this court's jurisdiction over Plaintiff was settled when Plaintiff filed his complaint invoking the court's jurisdiction. Whether the court jurisdiction over Strickland is something that must be resolved, if ever, by motion by Strickland, not Plaintiff.

The timing requirements of California Rules of Court, rule 3.110, including subsection (c), are enforced by way of order to show cause issued by the court (in this court, issued by Department 610). (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.110(f).) Thus, Plaintiff's motion to enforce those requirements in this department and absent an order to show cause issued by the court fails.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Notice & Motion For Discovery Request For Police Report(S) & Evidence Collected In Report 2309-0720
Off Calendar Mar-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF JOSEPH BANKS' Motion For Discovery Request For Police Report(S) & Evidence Collected In Report 2309-0720.

Plaintiff Joseph Alen Banks, Jr's Motion For Discovery Request For Police Report(S) & Evidence Collected In Report 2309-0720 against Defendant the BART Police Department is DENIED. The court construes Plaintiff's motion as a discovery motion to compel. Discovery in this court is governed by the Civil Discovery Act. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2016 et seq.) The California Civil Discovery Act sets out the rules and methods for a party to obtain material information from another. Those rules include several steps that must be taken before a party may seek an order compelling another to provide information. Here, the record does not show that Plaintiff has taken any of those steps . Thus, the court denies the motion. Plaintiff is advised to review and follow the Civil Discovery Act and associated rules.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and drop a hard paper copy in Dept. 302's inbox for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
